reasoning: Confirmed real. Hook chain: add_action('init', 'wpcaptcha::init', -1) fires on every page load with no auth gate; init() calls WPCaptcha_Functions::handle_unblock() unconditionally (advanced-google-recaptcha.php line 85). In handle_unblock() (functions.php lines 800-811), the only guard is a strict-equality match between $_GET['wpcaptcha_unblock'] and $options['global_unblock_key']; there is no nonce, no capability check, no session check — the code comment explicitly acknowledges this: 'no need for nonce check here as URL can be entered manually'. If the condition passes, the caller's IP is appended to the persistent whitelist via update_option(). The key ('agr'.md5(wp_generate_password(24)), 35 chars) is stored in wp_options and is intentionally exposed to logged-in admins via the AJAX recovery_url tool (ajax.php line 50) and emailed to locked-out users (functions.php ~line 751). An attacker who intercepts that email or obtains the key by any other means can send a single unauthenticated GET request from any IP to permanently whitelist it, bypassing all login-protection lockouts with no further interaction required. No sanitizer or capability guard was missed.
